description_long: This is a double-bladed axe featuring a robust wooden handle and two large, symmetrical metal blades affixed to either side of the head. The handle is slightly tapered, with a darker grip section near the base, suggesting reinforced wrapping or treatment for better handling. The metal blades are dark, with a rugged, matte finish and angular, almost geometric edges, giving the axe a formidable appearance. The head of the axe is secured with a metal collar, and the blades are broad, extending outward in a pronounced V-shape. There are no visible decorative engravings or branding, emphasizing a utilitarian design. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for heavy-duty use in urban or survival scenarios.
description: A double-bladed axe with a sturdy wooden handle and two large, angular metal blades, featuring a rugged, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The axe is shown with both blades clearly visible, highlighting the symmetrical, angular design and the robust wooden handle.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the thickness of the handle and the profile of the metal blades, showing the reinforced grip area.
description_view_2: A top-down perspective reveals the width of the axe head and the way the blades extend outward from the central collar.
description_view_3: The opposite side of the axe, again showing both blades and the full length of the handle, with the grip and collar details visible.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, tapered wooden handle with a darkened, possibly leather-wrapped grip section; round cross-section, ergonomic for two-handed use.
attribute_blade_design: Double-bladed, symmetrical, angular steel blades with a broad V-shape and rugged, matte finish; no beard or decorative features.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a metal collar securing the blades to the handle.
